title: WID Frontmatter
description: Schema for Work Intent Document YAML frontmatter. Validates the structured metadata block at the top of every WID file.

id.description: Unique identifier. Format: WID-NNN (zero-padded, minimum 3 digits). The authoritative identity of this WID — filenames and slugs are informational only.

title.description: Human-readable title. Should be concise but specific enough to distinguish from related WIDs.

type.description: Work type. Determines valid parent/child relationships and influences execution strategy.

status.description: Current lifecycle state. Typically written by the inference engine based on activity signals; humans rarely set this directly. Transitions: draft → ready → in-progress → review → done. 'cancelled' is reachable from any state.

created.description: Creation date in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD). Set once at WID creation, never modified.

author.description: Creator of the WID. Use @-prefixed handles (e.g., @ehammond, @claude-agent) for consistency with changelog entries.

parent.description: Parent WID in the hierarchy. An epic contains features/tasks; a feature contains tasks/bugs. Leaf types (task, bug, spike, chore) cannot be parents.

depends_on.description: WIDs that must reach 'done' before this WID can begin execution. Expresses execution ordering, separate from hierarchy.

blocks.description: WIDs that cannot begin until this WID reaches 'done'. Inverse of depends_on — maintained for convenience and cross-referencing.

priority.description: Execution priority. Used by the execution runtime to order the work queue when multiple WIDs are actionable.

labels.description: Free-form tags for filtering and grouping (e.g., 'auth', 'backend', 'tech-debt'). No controlled vocabulary — conventions emerge per project.

assignee.description: Current owner responsible for execution. Can be a human (@ehammond) or agent (@claude-agent). Blank means unassigned and available for pickup.

estimate.description: Estimated effort. Format: number + unit (m=minutes, h=hours, d=days). Examples: '30m', '2h', '0.5d'. Intentionally coarse — precise estimates are false precision.

due.description: Target completion date in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD). A goal, not a guarantee — useful for projection layer deadline views.

branch.description: Git branch associated with this WID's implementation. Convention: wid/WID-NNN-slug (e.g., 'wid/WID-001-oauth-google').

pr.description: Pull request URL(s) implementing this WID. String for a single PR, array for multiple (e.g., split PRs across repos).

context.description: Structured codebase context giving agents precise starting points and semantic descriptions that survive refactors.

path.description: Exact file path relative to repo root. Gives agents a precise starting point.

role.description: Semantic description of what this file does in the context of the WID. Survives renames — agents can relocate via search if the path moves.

glob.description: Broader glob pattern capturing related files (e.g., 'src/auth/oauth*.py'). Naturally includes new files matching the pattern.

description.description: What this module does and why it's relevant to the WID.
What this endpoint does and its relevance to the WID.

^x-.description: Extension fields. Use the x- prefix for project-specific custom metadata (e.g., x-team, x-sprint, x-cost-center). Keeps the core namespace clean while allowing arbitrary extensions.
